dimension: 起容器的机制对比 (Container-spawning mechanisms): DinD / DooD / Sysbox / Kata / podman / 宿主守护进程 — how a containerized CEO (or a non-Claude host helper) spawns one isolated container per agent, on macOS Docker Desktop today, while keeping ZERO Claude processes on the host.

options.name: DooD (Docker-out-of-Docker): bind-mount the host docker.sock into the CEO container — sibling containers
options.howItWorks: Run the CEO container with -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock plus the docker CLI inside. The CEO's `docker run` calls go to the HOST daemon (dockerd in the LinuxKit VM), which creates operator containers as SIBLINGS of the CEO (peers, not children). This is the de-facto pattern cua itself uses for its orchestration layer (multiple sibling desktop containers, orchestrator manages port allocation). On macOS the socket path is ~/.docker/run/docker.sock on the host, bridged into the LinuxKit VM; mount that path.
options.isolation: WEAK / dangerous. The docker.sock IS the root-equivalent control plane of the host daemon. Any process in the CEO container can `docker run --privileged -v /:/host ...` and own the whole daemon + every sibling + the LinuxKit VM. OWASP and Quarkslab both state sock access == unrestricted root on the daemon host. Read-only mount does NOT help (the API still creates privileged containers). So the CEO container is effectively trusted-as-root; the per-operator isolation between siblings is real (separate namespaces) but the CEO->host boundary is gone.
options.needsPrivileged: No --privileged on the CEO container itself, but functionally root-equivalent over the daemon — which is strictly WORSE than --privileged for blast radius (it can create privileged siblings on demand).
options.macFeasibility: WORKS TODAY on Docker Desktop for Mac. The LinuxKit VM exposes the daemon socket; bind-mounting ~/.docker/run/docker.sock is documented and routine. No host Claude needed: a tiny non-Claude launcher does one `docker run` to start the CEO container with the sock mounted, then exits. CEO (in-container) spawns every operator. This is the lowest-friction path to satisfying both hard constraints on a test Mac.
options.complexity: Lowest. ~1 line of run config + docker CLI in the image. No custom runtime, no host kernel changes.
pros: Satisfies 'each agent = its own container' (operators are real sibling containers) AND 'zero Claude on host' (only a one-shot non-Claude launcher touches the host).
Works on macOS Docker Desktop out of the box, no extra software.
Single shared image cache / fast pulls (all containers are peers on one daemon).
Matches how cua's own multi-container orchestration is built — low integration risk.
cons: CEO container is root-equivalent over the host daemon: a prompt-injected or buggy CEO can escape to the host / LinuxKit VM and nuke or hijack everything.
No isolation between the CEO and the host control plane — the single biggest security hole for an autonomous agent.
Operators are siblings, not children — the CEO 'manages' them via the host daemon, so a compromised host daemon compromises all agents at once.
Path/permission quirks on macOS (DOCKER_HOST, socket location moved across Docker Desktop versions).

options.name: True DinD (privileged dockerd INSIDE the CEO container) — child containers
options.howItWorks: Run the CEO container with --privileged and start a SECOND, independent dockerd inside it (the classic docker:dind image). The CEO's docker CLI talks to its OWN inner daemon; operator containers are CHILDREN nested inside the CEO container, fully separate from the host daemon. The inner daemon stores images in the CEO container's filesystem.
options.isolation: Inner daemon is separate from the host daemon (good: host daemon is not exposed). BUT --privileged grants the CEO container nearly all host capabilities, device access, and the ability to interact with the host kernel — a well-known escape surface (cgroup release_agent, /sys writes, kernel-module load). On Docker Desktop the 'host' it can attack is the LinuxKit VM, which is a partial mitigation but not a guarantee. 8 container-escape CVEs shipped 2024-2025 (Leaky Vessels, runc races, NVIDIAScape) — privileged amplifies all of them.
options.needsPrivileged: YES, --privileged (or a large hand-picked --cap-add + device set). This is the headline drawback.
options.macFeasibility: WORKS on Docker Desktop for Mac (docker:dind + --privileged runs inside the LinuxKit VM). Nested overlayfs/storage works. Non-Claude launcher starts the privileged CEO; CEO runs its own dockerd and spawns operators. Satisfies both hard constraints (operators are child containers; zero host Claude).
options.complexity: Medium. Need dind image, --privileged, often a vfs/overlay storage tweak, and each CEO carries its own image cache (duplicated pulls, more disk — see the Docker 4.58 'crazy disk usage' reports for the same class of problem).
pros: Operators are children of the CEO container — clean 'each agent = own container nested under its owner' model; tearing down the CEO removes its whole subtree.
Host daemon is NOT exposed (unlike DooD) — no docker.sock on the host is shared.
Self-contained: the CEO image fully owns its agent fleet; portable to Linux prod later.
Zero Claude on host (launcher is non-Claude).
cons: --privileged is a real escape vector to the LinuxKit VM; not acceptable for untrusted/agent-generated actions long-term.
Duplicated image storage per CEO; heavier disk/RAM, slower cold start.
Nested storage drivers can be finicky; occasional overlay-on-overlay issues.
Each operator inside still shares the inner daemon — operator-to-operator isolation is only namespace-level, same as plain containers.

options.name: Sysbox runtime (secure, rootless, unprivileged DinD) — the 'right' DinD
options.howItWorks: Sysbox is a next-gen OCI runtime (sysbox-runc) installed ON THE HOST. You run the CEO as `docker run --runtime=sysbox-runc ...`; inside it you install/run a normal dockerd and spawn operators as children — but with NO --privileged and NO sock mount. Sysbox uses the Linux user-namespace (root-in-container maps to an unprivileged host user), procfs/sysfs virtualization, and syscall trapping so nested Docker 'just works' and is fully isolated from the host daemon.
options.isolation: STRONG for a shared-kernel approach. Inner Docker is totally isolated from host Docker; a --privileged container created INSIDE the Sysbox container is privileged only within that container, with no host privileges. root in container != root on host. This is exactly the security posture you want for an autonomous CEO that spawns agents. Still shares the host kernel (not a VM boundary), so kernel-level 0-days remain the theoretical ceiling — but the practical escape surface is far smaller than DinD/DooD.
options.needsPrivileged: NO. That is the entire point — secure DinD without --privileged and without docker.sock.
options.macFeasibility: BLOCKED on macOS Docker Desktop. Sysbox must be installed as a runtime on the Linux HOST and registers with that host's dockerd; it requires a supported Linux distro/kernel and (for the deb install) systemd. Docker Desktop's daemon runs inside the opaque LinuxKit VM, which you cannot install sysbox-runc into in a supported way. So on the test Mac this is NOT viable. It becomes the recommended option ONLY once you move to a real Linux host (prod / a Linux build box / a Linux VM you control).
options.complexity: Low to USE (once installed), Medium to INSTALL — but installation is impossible-in-practice on Docker Desktop Mac; trivial on Linux (apt install, or the Docker-sponsored package since Nestybox joined Docker).
pros: Best security/usability tradeoff for shared-kernel: unprivileged, rootless, no sock — directly removes the DooD/DinD escape vectors.
Inner daemon fully isolated from host daemon; nested privileged-in-container is contained.
Operators are clean children; CEO owns its subtree; behaves like a VM for the agent.
Backed by Docker (acquired Nestybox); actively patched (ported runc CVE fixes 2025).
cons: Cannot run on macOS Docker Desktop (LinuxKit VM) for the test phase — this is the deal-breaker NOW.
Linux-host only; needs host-level runtime install + kernel support (5.x+, ideally newer).
Still a shared-kernel boundary, not a hardware VM boundary.
Adds an operational dependency (host must carry the sysbox runtime) in prod.

options.name: Kata Containers (VM-isolated, one lightweight VM per container)
options.howItWorks: Kata is an OCI runtime that boots each container inside its own lightweight VM (QEMU / Cloud Hypervisor / Firecracker) with its own guest kernel, dropping into containerd/Docker via --runtime. Each operator (and the CEO) would get hardware-level isolation. For DinD-style spawning you'd combine Kata as the runtime with a daemon that creates Kata containers.
options.isolation: STRONGEST among these (hardware virtualization = genuine boundary; hypervisor escapes are rare, $250K-500K bounties). Each agent gets its own guest kernel, so a container escape stays inside that agent's VM. This is the gold standard for untrusted/agent code.
options.needsPrivileged: No --privileged for the workload, but REQUIRES nested virtualization (VT-x/AMD-V exposed to the layer running Kata) and a Kata-enabled runtime on the host.
options.macFeasibility: BLOCKED inside Docker Desktop. Kata needs nested virt that Docker Desktop's LinuxKit VM does not expose, and Kata's runtime can't be installed into that VM. IMPORTANT nuance: Apple's own Containerization framework (WWDC 2025) and Docker Desktop 4.58's microVM sandbox are effectively 'Kata-for-macOS' — they give each container/agent its OWN microVM via Apple virtualization.framework. So the Kata *concept* IS available on macOS, just through Docker's/Apple's native microVM path (see next option), not through upstream Kata.
options.complexity: High (upstream Kata: nested virt, runtime install, host tuning). The macOS-native equivalent (Docker 4.58 microVM) is far simpler to consume.
pros: True per-agent hardware isolation — the only option that makes a compromised agent unable to touch siblings/host even with a kernel 0-day.
Industry direction for AI-agent sandboxes (E2B/Fly use Firecracker microVMs; Daytona offers Kata as an option).
Maps perfectly to 'each agent = its own (micro)VM-backed container'.
cons: Upstream Kata cannot run inside Docker Desktop on the Mac (no nested virt, no runtime install).
Higher per-agent overhead (150-300ms boot, more RAM) than shared-kernel containers.
Operationally complex on real Linux; needs bare-metal or nested-virt-capable hosts.
GPU/feature passthrough caveats depending on hypervisor backend.

options.name: podman rootless / nested (DinD-like without a daemon or sock)
options.howItWorks: Podman is daemonless. The CEO image runs podman; operators are spawned as nested rootless containers (podman-in-podman) using user namespaces + fuse-overlayfs (or native rootless overlay), needing only /dev/fuse and subuid/subgid setup — NO docker.sock, NO separate daemon. The CEO process itself forks/execs operator containers.
options.isolation: Good for a shared-kernel model: rootless means container-root maps to an unprivileged host user (like Sysbox's property), no daemon socket to leak, smaller attack surface than DooD/DinD. Still shared kernel. Operators are children of the CEO's podman.
options.needsPrivileged: No --privileged in the ideal path; rootless nested needs /dev/fuse + proper subuid/subgid and a kernel that allows unprivileged user namespaces. In practice on constrained hosts people sometimes fall back to --privileged for the OUTER container, which erodes the benefit.
options.macFeasibility: PARTIAL on Docker Desktop. Podman-machine on Mac is its own LinuxKit-like VM; running podman-in-podman INSIDE a Docker-Desktop container is possible but the rootless/fuse path is fragile (needs /dev/fuse exposed, kernel user-ns enabled in the LinuxKit VM, which is not guaranteed). Often degrades to needing --privileged on the outer container, at which point it's no better than DinD. Viable but finicky; not the smooth path on a Mac.
options.complexity: Medium-High on Mac (fuse-overlayfs, subuid/subgid, /dev/fuse, user-ns flags); fuse-overlayfs also has ~2x I/O overhead.
pros: Daemonless + rootless: no docker.sock to leak, no host daemon exposed — removes the #1 DooD risk.
Operators are clean nested children of the CEO; CEO owns its fleet.
Best-in-class for 'unprivileged nested containers' when on a cooperative Linux host.
Zero Claude on host (launcher starts the podman-based CEO).
cons: Rootless nested is fragile on Docker Desktop's LinuxKit VM (user-ns / /dev/fuse not reliably available) — often forces --privileged, defeating the purpose.
fuse-overlayfs performance penalty (~2x) for image-heavy operators.
Your existing image (foundagent/cua-agent) is Docker-based; switching the spawn mechanism to podman is a real porting cost.
Still shared-kernel; no VM boundary.

options.name: Host-side non-Claude orchestrator daemon (launcher process that docker-runs operators on command) — incl. Docker Desktop 4.58 sandboxd microVM API
options.howItWorks: A small non-Claude daemon runs on the host (e.g., a Go/Python service, NOT Claude). It listens on a private channel (a Unix socket / local HTTP) that the CEO container can reach but which exposes ONLY a narrow API: 'spawn operator with image X, mounts Y', 'stop operator Z', 'list'. The daemon translates those into `docker run`/`docker stop` against the host daemon. The CEO never touches docker.sock directly — it only speaks the restricted verb set. On macOS this pattern is now PRODUCTIZED: Docker Desktop 4.58 (Jan 2026) ships a `sandboxd` daemon at ~/.docker/sandboxes/sandboxd.sock with GET/POST/DELETE /vm — each POST boots a per-agent microVM (Apple virtualization.framework) with its OWN private Docker daemon, fully isolated from the host and from sibling VMs. That is exactly 'host-side launcher + per-agent VM-isolated container', already built and tuned for AI agents.
options.isolation: Strongest practical posture for THIS project. The CEO is NOT root-equivalent over the host (unlike DooD) — it can only invoke whitelisted verbs; the host daemon sits behind your policy code. With the Docker 4.58 microVM path, each operator additionally gets HARDWARE VM isolation (own kernel) — agent escape stays inside that agent's microVM. Caveat to verify: the 4.58 sandboxd.sock is currently HOST-invoked; it's undocumented whether a process INSIDE a microVM can call back to spawn siblings, so the CEO likely spawns operators via YOUR thin daemon (which holds the sandboxd/docker access), keeping the privileged surface on the host helper, not in the CEO.
options.needsPrivileged: No --privileged on agent containers. The privileged surface (docker.sock or sandboxd.sock access) lives ONLY in the small, auditable host daemon you control — never in a Claude-bearing container. The host daemon is non-Claude, so it does not violate 'zero Claude on host'.
options.macFeasibility: BEST FIT on the test Mac. Two concrete sub-paths: (a) write a ~100-line non-Claude broker that holds the host docker.sock and exposes only spawn/stop/list to the CEO container over a socket — works on any Docker Desktop today; (b) point that broker at Docker Desktop 4.58's sandboxd microVM API to get per-agent Apple-VM isolation for free. Both keep zero Claude on host (broker is non-Claude) and give each agent its own container/VM.
options.complexity: Medium. You write+maintain a small broker (auth, verb whitelist, lifecycle, cleanup). The 4.58 microVM API is undocumented and 'subject to change' (reverse-engineered by Rivet), so pin the Docker Desktop version if you depend on it; otherwise the plain-docker broker is stable.
pros: Cleanly satisfies BOTH hard constraints: agents are isolated containers (optionally VM-isolated), and the ONLY host process is your non-Claude broker — zero Claude on host.
Removes the DooD root-equivalence from the CEO: the dangerous capability is quarantined in a tiny, auditable, policy-enforcing daemon you own.
On macOS can ride Docker 4.58 sandboxd to get per-agent Apple-virtualization-framework microVMs (hardware isolation) without building Kata yourself.
Maps 1:1 to a future Linux/prod design (swap the broker's backend from Docker-Desktop-sandboxd to plain docker / Sysbox / Kata without changing the CEO contract).
Lets you enforce quotas, egress policy, naming, and teardown centrally.
cons: You must build and secure the broker (a compromised broker == DooD-level blast radius, so its API surface must be minimal and authenticated).
The 4.58 microVM API is undocumented/unstable; nested invocation (CEO-in-VM spawning sibling VMs) is unconfirmed, so the broker, not the CEO, must drive it.
Slightly more moving parts than a raw sock mount.
If you skip the microVM path and just broker plain docker, isolation between agents is still only namespace-level (shared kernel).

recommendation:
RECOMMENDATION — split by phase, because macOS Docker Desktop's LinuxKit VM rules out the textbook 'right answers' (Sysbox, upstream Kata) during the test phase.

FOR THE TEST MAC (now): Do NOT mount the raw host docker.sock into the CEO container (DooD) and do NOT run a privileged DinD CEO — both make the Claude-bearing CEO root-equivalent over the host/LinuxKit VM, which is exactly what you must not give an autonomous, prompt-injectable agent. Instead adopt the HOST-SIDE NON-CLAUDE BROKER pattern (option 6): a tiny ~100-line non-Claude daemon on the Mac holds the host docker access and exposes ONLY a narrow {spawn, stop, list} API to the CEO container over a private socket. This satisfies both hard constraints exactly — every agent (CEO + each operator) is its own isolated container, and the only thing on the host is your non-Claude broker (zero Claude on host). The CEO 'manages operators' by calling the broker, not by holding docker.sock. The broker creates each operator as a sibling container running your existing foundagent/cua-agent image.

To answer the user's direct question 'do we need containers-nested-in-containers / DinD?': NO. You do not need DinD or DinD-of-any-kind. The cleanest model is FLAT sibling containers created by a host broker, with the CEO and operators all being peers managed through that broker. Nesting (DinD/Sysbox/podman-in-podman) only buys you a 'subtree teardown' convenience that the broker can replicate by tracking ownership, and on the Mac the nesting options are either insecure (privileged DinD) or unavailable (Sysbox/Kata).

ISOLATION UPGRADE on the Mac (optional, strong): point the broker at Docker Desktop 4.58's sandboxd microVM API (~/.docker/sandboxes/sandboxd.sock). Each agent then gets its own Apple-virtualization.framework microVM with a private Docker daemon — real hardware isolation per agent, essentially 'Kata for macOS' for free. Caveat: that API is undocumented/unstable and is host-invoked (nested call-back from inside a VM is unconfirmed), so keep the broker as the only caller and pin the Docker Desktop version. If you want stability over max-isolation for the first milestone, ship the plain-docker broker first and add the microVM backend behind the same broker interface later.

FOR LINUX / PROD (later, do not paint into a corner): keep the broker's CEO-facing contract identical and swap the backend. On a real Linux host the correct secure-nesting answer is Sysbox (unprivileged, rootless, no sock, isolated inner Docker) for cost-efficient density, and Kata/Firecracker microVMs when you need true hardware isolation per agent (the E2B/Fly/Daytona direction). Because the CEO only ever talks to the broker, migrating from Docker-Desktop-sandboxd -> Sysbox -> Kata requires zero CEO changes.

NET: Broker pattern now (flat siblings, no DinD), optionally backed by Docker 4.58 microVMs for per-agent VM isolation on the Mac; Sysbox or Kata behind the same broker contract in Linux prod. Avoid raw docker.sock-in-CEO and privileged DinD entirely.
keyInsights: macOS Docker Desktop's architecture (daemon runs inside an opaque LinuxKit VM) is the decisive constraint: it eliminates Sysbox (host-only Linux runtime install) and upstream Kata (needs nested virt the LinuxKit VM does not expose) for the TEST phase, even though both are the 'right' answers on Linux.

You do NOT need Docker-in-Docker at all. A host-side non-Claude broker creating FLAT sibling containers satisfies 'each agent = own container' + 'zero Claude on host' more securely than any nesting scheme. Nesting only adds escape risk (privileged DinD) or unavailability (Sysbox/Kata) on the Mac.

Mounting host docker.sock into the CEO (DooD) is root-equivalent over the host daemon — OWASP/Quarkslab: it's effectively WORSE than --privileged because the CEO can spawn privileged siblings and read /:/host. For an autonomous, prompt-injectable CEO this is the single worst choice; never put docker.sock inside a Claude-bearing container.

Docker Desktop 4.58 (Jan 2026) shipped a real game-changer: a host sandboxd daemon (~/.docker/sandboxes/sandboxd.sock, GET/POST/DELETE /vm) that boots a per-agent microVM via Apple virtualization.framework, each with its OWN private Docker daemon, isolated from host and siblings. This is 'Kata-for-macOS' and is purpose-built for AI agents — but the API is undocumented/unstable and host-invoked (nested call-back unconfirmed), so a broker should be the sole caller and the version pinned.

Containers are NOT a security boundary — 8 container-escape CVEs in 2024-2025 (Leaky Vessels, runc races, NVIDIAScape). For untrusted/agent-generated actions the industry has converged on the 'Matryoshka' model: container-inside-microVM (E2B/Fly = Firecracker, Daytona offers Kata, Docker 4.58 = Apple VM). Plan toward per-agent VM isolation, not just namespaces.

Design the CEO->spawn interface as a stable broker contract NOW so the backend can swap without touching agents: Docker-Desktop-sandboxd (Mac test) -> Sysbox (Linux density) -> Kata/Firecracker (Linux max-isolation). This is the no-corner-painting move.

podman rootless-nested is the cleanest shared-kernel nesting on Linux (daemonless, no sock, root-in-container maps to unprivileged host user), but on Docker Desktop's LinuxKit VM it's fragile (needs /dev/fuse + unprivileged user-ns) and often degrades to --privileged, plus it'd mean porting your Docker-based cua image.

dimension: Foundagent 编排架构契合（CEO 容器 vs 宿主守护进程 vs 同容器多会话）— 在「每 agent 一容器 + 宿主零 Claude 进程 + CEO（容器内）需驱动 operator 容器」约束下，CEO 如何编排 operator 容器的拓扑选型

options.name: Topology A — DooD via mounted docker.sock（CEO 容器挂宿主 docker socket，跑 sibling operator 容器）
options.howItWorks: CEO 容器 = foundagent/cua-agent 之上加装 docker CLI；启动时 -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock。CEO 内的 Claude（订阅）调一个薄 orchestrator MCP（封装 cua 的 Computer(provider_type='docker', image='foundagent/cua-agent', name='op-<id>').run()），cua provider 纯 subprocess 调 docker run/stop/rm/inspect，命中宿主 docker daemon。daemon 在 CEO 旁创建 operator 为【兄弟容器】（非嵌套），与 CEO 同级。operator 内已是完整 Claude-in-container（claude CLI 订阅 + CUA_HOST_SERVER=1 接 localhost:8000 computer-server），自跑 CU 循环。生命周期：create=docker run -d --name op-<id>；teardown=docker stop+rm；状态=docker inspect/ps。宿主零 Claude 进程（CEO 与 operator 全在容器内；docker daemon 是宿主上的非-Claude 进程，本就允许）。
options.complexity: 低（复用已验证的 cua docker provider + 已验证的 cua-agent 镜像；只需给 CEO 镜像加 docker CLI + 挂 socket）
options.needsPrivileged: 不需要 --privileged，但需挂 docker.sock（= 对该 daemon 的 root 等效控制）
options.macFeasibility: 高。Docker Desktop 的 sock 挂载是一等公民；escape 只落到 LinuxKit VM（宿主 Mac 受 VM 边界保护），非落到 Mac
options.isolation: 容器级（runc + Docker Desktop LinuxKit VM 兜底）。operator 之间互不可见仅靠 Docker 默认网络/命名空间隔离；CEO 持 socket = 对所有兄弟容器的完全控制
pros: 与现状改动最小：cua provider 是纯 docker CLI subprocess，CEO 容器内挂 socket 后【逐字复用】host 上已跑通的 spike 路径（Computer(...).run()）
operator = 兄弟容器（非嵌套），无嵌套虚拟化/存储驱动叠层开销，性能与直接在宿主跑等价
完美契合『每 agent 一容器 + 宿主零 Claude 进程』：CEO 和所有 operator 都在容器内，宿主只剩 docker daemon（非 Claude）
CEO→部门→执行者天然映射：CEO 容器 docker run 出 operator 容器；结果经 operator 退出码 + 落盘 transcript/卷回流
macOS 上 docker.sock escape 的受害面是 LinuxKit VM 而非 Mac（官方文档明示 VM 是 host 安全边界）
cons: 持有 docker.sock = 对该 daemon 的 root 等效权限：被攻陷的 CEO 容器可创建 --privileged 容器、挂 LinuxKit VM 根文件系统、控制所有兄弟容器（OWASP/Datadog 明确反对裸挂 sock）
operator 与 CEO 是兄弟而非真父子：bind-mount 路径以【宿主/LinuxKit VM】为基准而非 CEO 容器内路径，易踩共享卷路径坑
Docker Desktop 4.38.0（本机）低于 4.44.3 → 受 CVE-2025-9074 影响：任意容器可在【不挂 sock】下经 192.168.65.7:2375 直达 Engine API（即隔离假象，须先升级 Docker Desktop）
无内核级硬隔离（runc 共享内核）；测试期可接受，生产跨主机需换控制面

options.name: Topology A-hardened — DooD via docker-socket-proxy（CEO 经【受限 TCP 代理】访问 daemon，最小权限）
options.howItWorks: 在 A 基础上不直接挂 sock：跑一个 tecnativa/docker-socket-proxy sidecar（它挂真 sock），仅开 CONTAINERS=1 POST=1 ALLOW_START=1 ALLOW_STOP=1 IMAGES=1（读）；其余（EXEC/privileged/secrets/host-bind）一律拒。CEO 容器设 DOCKER_HOST=tcp://docker-proxy:2375，docker CLI / cua provider 透明走代理。CEO 只能 create/start/stop/inspect 容器，【无法】创建 privileged 容器或挂宿主目录 → 把『root 等效』收敛成『只能管 operator 生命周期』。
options.complexity: 中（多一个 proxy sidecar + 网络 + 端点白名单；cua provider 因走 DOCKER_HOST 仍零改动）
options.needsPrivileged: 不需要；proxy 容器持 sock，CEO 不持
options.macFeasibility: 高（proxy 镜像 arm64 可用；纯网络转发）
options.isolation: 容器级 + 【API 层最小权限】：即使 CEO 被攻陷也只能在白名单端点内操作，挡掉 privileged/host-mount 提权链
pros: 保留 A 的『改动最小、复用 cua provider』全部优点，且 cua provider 因 DOCKER_HOST 走代理而零改动
把『持 sock=root 等效』收敛为『只能管 operator 生命周期』，堵住 privileged 容器 + 宿主目录挂载提权链（业界标准缓解）
POST 默认关、按端点显式放行 → 显式、可审计的编排能力边界，契合零人公司长期自治的安全姿态
为生产留好路：代理可平滑替换为远程受限 daemon / 真控制面 API
cons: proxy 仅过滤 API 端点，不阻止『被允许的能力本身被滥用』（如批量起 operator 烧资源）——需配额/数量上限补
多一个常驻 sidecar + 一段网络路径（排障面增加）
仍非内核级硬隔离；CVE-2025-9074 这类【绕过 sock 直连 Engine API】的洞由 proxy 无法防（须靠升级 Docker Desktop ≥4.44.3 + 开 ECI）

options.name: Topology B — 宿主非-Claude launcher daemon（CEO 经文件队列/本地 socket/HTTP 请求 operator，daemon 代起容器）
options.howItWorks: 宿主跑一个 python/go 写的【非 Claude】launcher daemon（=控制面，约 100-200 行），它持有 docker 访问权。CEO 容器【不持 docker 访问】，只通过共享卷文件队列（jobs/<id>.json）或本地 unix socket / 回环 HTTP 向 daemon 投递『起 operator: image/identity/task』意图。daemon 校验后 docker run operator 容器，把 status/结果写回 results/<id>.json 或 SSE。operator 仍是 Claude-in-container 自跑 CU。映射：daemon=确定性调度内核（类似 v5 DAG 调度器 / E2B orchestrator），CEO=发任务的『plan』节点，operator=执行者。
options.complexity: 中高（要自写并维护 daemon：队列/幂等/超时/回收/鉴权/崩溃恢复，是 load-bearing 组件须单测）
options.needsPrivileged: CEO 完全不需 docker 权限（最干净）；daemon 在宿主持 docker（非 Claude，符合约束）
options.macFeasibility: 高（纯宿主进程 + 共享卷/本地端口，Mac 上稳）
options.isolation: 最强能力隔离：CEO 容器【完全无 docker 访问】，只能投递结构化意图；daemon 是唯一可起容器者，能力面由 daemon 代码精确界定（白名单镜像、数量上限、resource 配额、身份绑定）
pros: 最符合『宿主零 Claude 进程』的【精神】：宿主只剩一个非 Claude 控制面 daemon；CEO 零 docker 特权 → 攻陷 CEO 也碰不到 docker daemon
控制面/数据面分离，与 E2B(orchestrator+host nodes)、v5(DAG 调度器+lane) 的成熟范式一致 → 生产/跨主机演进【零返工】（daemon 换成远程 API 即可）
CEO→部门→执行者契合最佳：daemon 天然是放配额/审批/审计/kill-switch/可观测聚合的地方（契合 v6 北极星的『kill 决策』机制落点）
幂等队列 + 结果回写 = 崩溃恢复友好（CEO 重启后从队列/结果续跑），契合 VM8 graceful + resume
cons: 要自写并长期维护一个真 daemon（队列协议、超时回收、鉴权、崩溃恢复）——比 A 多可观工程量，且是必须正经测试的关键组件
cua provider 不能直接用（它要本地 docker CLI）：需在 daemon 侧封装容器生命周期，或让 daemon 调 cua provider，CEO 侧重写一个『投递意图』的薄 MCP
多一跳（CEO→daemon→docker）增加延迟与排障面；文件队列要处理并发/部分写/孤儿 job
测试期『最小可运行』成本高于 A：A 当天能跑通，B 要先把 daemon 写对

options.name: Topology C — CEO + operators 为【同一容器内多 Claude 会话】（多 claude 进程/多 worktree）
options.howItWorks: 单个 foundagent/cua-agent 容器内跑多个 claude 会话：CEO 一个会话，operator 各为该容器内的子进程/子会话（如 Trellis channel 扇出 + git worktree，或多 `claude -p` 子进程）。共享同一桌面/同一 computer-server/同一身份。
options.complexity: 低（无需 docker 编排；但与硬约束冲突）
options.isolation: 【不满足约束】：所有会话共享同一容器=同一内核命名空间、同一桌面、同一 secrets/身份、同一出口 IP。一个会话被攻陷或越权即污染全员
pros: 资源最省、启动最快、无任何 docker 编排复杂度
适合『同一身份内的纯文本子任务扇出』（如 Trellis subagent 做规划/写码，不需独立桌面/身份时）
cons: 【直接违反硬约束『每 agent session 独立容器』】：operator 与 CEO 同容器，非 per-agent-container
无身份/出口 IP/secrets 隔离 → 养号致命（一个会话的指纹/IP 关联污染所有身份），与 infra 研究『每身份固定独享 IP』结论冲突
computer-use 资源争用：同一桌面被多会话同时点像素 → 互相打架，无法并发跑多 operator 各自的 GUI 任务
可观测/graceful 难：多会话挤一份日志/一个 PID1，崩溃相互牵连

recommendation:
主推：先 Topology A-hardened（docker-socket-proxy 收敛的 DooD）作为测试期落地，并把编排意图通过一个薄『orchestrator MCP』封装，使其能在 Phase 2 平滑升级为 Topology B（宿主非-Claude 控制面 daemon）。一句话：A-hardened 是『今天就能跑通且复用已验证组件』的最短路径，B 是『生产/跨主机不返工』的终态，两者用同一个 MCP 抽象桥接。

为什么不是裸 A：本机 Docker Desktop 是 4.38.0（< 4.44.3），当前受 CVE-2025-9074 影响（任意容器【不挂 sock】即可经 192.168.65.7:2375 直连 Engine API）——这意味着『容器隔离』在升级前是假象。落地第一步必须：①升级 Docker Desktop ≥ 4.44.3；②（Business 订阅可用时）开 Enhanced Container Isolation（ECI 用 Sysbox+user namespace，默认阻断裸挂 docker.sock，可对受信镜像开例外）。

为什么不是直接上 B：B 是架构正解，但要自写并测试一个真 daemon（队列/超时/回收/鉴权/崩溃恢复），违背用户『先最小可运行、再测试驱动扩展』的方法论。A-hardened 当天即可复用已验证的 cua docker provider + cua-agent 镜像跑通；B 留作 Phase 2。

为什么否决 C：直接违反『每 agent 一容器』硬约束，且无身份/IP/secrets 隔离，与 infra 研究『每身份固定独享 ISP IP 是养号命脉』结论正面冲突。C 仅适合『同身份内的纯文本子任务扇出』（如 Trellis subagent 规划/写码），不能用于需独立桌面+身份+出口 IP 的 operator。

落地形态（CEO→部门→执行者）：
- CEO 容器 = foundagent/cua-agent + docker CLI；设 DOCKER_HOST=tcp://docker-proxy:2375。
- docker-socket-proxy sidecar 持真 sock，仅放行 CONTAINERS/POST/ALLOW_START/ALLOW_STOP/IMAGES(读)，拒 EXEC/privileged/host-bind。
- CEO 内 Claude（订阅）调薄 orchestrator MCP：spawn_operator(identity, task) → 内部走 cua Computer(provider_type='docker', image='foundagent/cua-agent', name='op-<id>').run()（逐字复用 spike）；operator 起为 CEO 的兄弟容器，自跑 Claude-in-container CU 循环。
- 结果/状态回流：operator 退出码 + 落盘 JSONL transcript/结果文件（经命名卷 results/op-<id>/）+ docker inspect 轮询状态；CEO 只收成败+摘要+终态截图（契合已定的双层 computer-use）。
- teardown：MCP 调 docker stop+rm；身份/卷按 per-identity 命名（op-<id>）便于回收。
- 升级到 B：把 orchestrator MCP 的后端从『本地走 proxy 的 docker CLI』换成『向宿主 daemon 投递 job』，CEO 侧 MCP 接口不变 → 零返工跨主机。
keyInsights: 不需要『容器套容器(真 DinD/嵌套 daemon)』。正解是 DooD（sibling 容器）：CEO 容器经 docker.sock(或受限代理)让【宿主同一个 daemon】在 CEO 旁边创建 operator 兄弟容器，二者同级、非嵌套——既满足『每 agent 一容器』又避免嵌套虚拟化/存储叠层开销。
cua 的 Docker provider 是纯 `docker` CLI subprocess（run/stop/rm/inspect/ps，已读源码 provider.py 验证）→ 只要 CEO 容器内能触达 daemon（挂 sock 或 DOCKER_HOST=tcp://proxy:2375），host 上跑通的 spike 路径在容器内【逐字复用】，这是 A 改动极小的根因。
『宿主零 Claude 进程』在所有可行拓扑中都成立：docker daemon / launcher daemon / socket-proxy 都是【非 Claude】进程，本就被约束允许；CEO 与 operator 全在容器内。约束真正排除的是 Topology C（多会话挤一容器）。

macOS 安全边界关键事实（官方文档）：Docker Desktop 容器跑在 LinuxKit Linux VM 内，VM 本身是 host 安全边界——容器即便 root 也不等于 Mac root，docker.sock escape 落点是 LinuxKit VM 而非 Mac。这使测试期裸 DooD 的『宿主沦陷』风险显著低于 Linux 裸机，但 VM 内的横向污染（控制所有兄弟容器）仍真实存在。
CVE-2025-9074（CVSS 9.3，已在 4.44.3 修复）是本机【当前真实】风险：Docker Desktop < 4.44.3 上任意容器【无需挂 sock】即可经 192.168.65.7:2375 无认证直达 Engine API，开不开 ECI 都中招。本机 4.38.0 中招 → 落地前必须升级，否则『隔离』是假象。
强隔离 runtime 在 Mac 上的现实：Kata Containers【无法】在 Docker Desktop 跑（需嵌套虚拟化，LinuxKit VM 不暴露）；gVisor(runsc) 可装但加 syscall 拦截开销且与桌面/GUI 兼容性需验。测试期靠『runc + LinuxKit VM 兜底』足够；要硬隔离走 cua Lume micro-VM 或 ECI(Sysbox)。
业界范式佐证 B 是终态：E2B = orchestrator 节点 + Firecracker host 节点；Daytona = 容器(可选 Kata/Sysbox)+ 支持 DinD；v5 自身 = 确定性 DAG 调度器 + lane。共性是『控制面/数据面分离、调度器是放配额/审计/kill 的地方』——正是 v6 CEO→部门→执行者 + 自主 kill 决策的天然落点。
docker-socket-proxy(tecnativa) 把『持 sock=root 等效』收敛为最小权限：POST 默认关、按端点(CONTAINERS/ALLOW_START/ALLOW_STOP)显式放行，挡掉 privileged 容器+宿主目录挂载这两条主要提权链；cua provider 因走 DOCKER_HOST 仍零改动——这是测试期『安全且改动最小』的甜点。
现有 foundagent/cua-agent 镜像有 claude CLI + node 但【无 docker CLI】(已实测)。Topology A 只需在【CEO 镜像】加 docker CLI(operator 镜像不需要)，operator 镜像保持现状不动 → 复用最大化。

相关文件：/Users/weston/dev/BuildFactory/vm/cua_mcp.py（薄 MCP，已支持 host/container 双模式，可扩出 spawn_operator）、/Users/weston/dev/BuildFactory/vm/docker/Dockerfile.agent（operator 镜像，CEO 镜像在此基础上加 docker CLI）、/Users/weston/dev/BuildFactory/.venv-cua/lib/python3.13/site-packages/computer/providers/docker/provider.py（cua docker provider 源码，确认纯 CLI subprocess）。

dimension: Zero-person company 的安全 / 隔离模型 (security & isolation model: per-agent containers, zero host Claude, untrusted web + real credentials)
recommendation:
回答用户的核心问题: 不需要 docker-in-docker (DinD)。正确架构是 "siblings, not nesting" —— 宿主上一个非-Claude 的 launcher daemon 启动 CEO 容器; CEO 通过一个**受限 broker API**(不是裸 docker.sock)请求 launcher 创建 operator 容器, 这些 operator 是 CEO 的**兄弟容器**(都由宿主 dockerd 创建), 而不是嵌套在 CEO 里面。这同时满足两个硬约束: (1) 运行时宿主上零 Claude 进程(CEO+operator 全在容器内), (2) CEO 能 "spawn/manage" operator(经 broker 调用宿主 dockerd)。

绝对不要做的两件事: (a) 把裸 /var/run/docker.sock 挂进 CEO 容器 —— 这等于把宿主 root 交给一个会读取不可信网页(prompt-injection)、且持有真实账号/钱的 agent, 一次注入即可 `docker run -v /:/host` 拿下整个 Mac; (b) 用 `--privileged` DinD —— privileged 容器能 load kernel module / 访问宿主设备, 逃逸是 "简单" 而非 "需要 0-day" 的。

推荐的分层方案:

**Mac 测试期 (现在, 务实版):**
1. 宿主 launcher daemon(Go/Python, 非 Claude)持有 dockerd 访问权, 对外只暴露一个**窄 spawn API**(只允许 create/start/stop/logs 指定 image+固定安全模板, 不允许任意 bind-mount、不允许 privileged、不允许改 network mode)。CEO 容器调用这个 API, 而不是 docker.sock。若想省事可用 tecnativa/docker-socket-proxy 限制 API, 但**它无法限制 POST /containers/create 的参数体**(仍可注入 `-v /:/host`), 所以高安全场景必须用自写的语义化 broker, 而不是 socket-proxy。
2. 每个 operator 一个独立容器(已符合要求), 容器内非 root 用户、`--cap-drop ALL`、`--security-opt no-new-privileges`、只读 rootfs + tmpfs、seccomp 默认 profile。
3. **网络 egress 白名单**是对抗 prompt-injection 数据外泄的第一道也是最有效的一道防线: operator 容器走一个 egress 代理/防火墙, 只放行任务必需域名(注入成功但无法把数据发到攻击者域名, 外泄即失败)。
4. **凭据隔离**: 每个 operator 只拿到它身份对应的、最小权限、可随时吊销的凭据(per-identity), 绝不共享一个 "全能" 凭据; 凭据通过短期 token / 运行时注入, 不写进 image, 容器间无横向移动路径。
5. 强烈建议开 **Docker Desktop Enhanced Container Isolation (ECI)**: 它在 LinuxKit VM 内用 Sysbox 做 user-namespace 映射(容器 root→VM 内非特权 UID), 默认**阻止挂载 docker.sock**、即使 `--privileged` 也无法 load kernel module / 改宿主网络。ECI 在 macOS Docker Desktop 可用, 但**需要 Docker Business 付费订阅**。这是 Mac 期最高性价比的纵深防御。

**生产期 (未来, 别把自己逼进死角):**
- 把 "宿主 dockerd" 换成真正的 per-session 强隔离 runtime。优先级: Firecracker microVM (E2B 用的, 每 session 独立 kernel, 逃逸需 hypervisor escape, 业界对 "不可信代码" 的 gold standard) 或 Kata Containers (Daytona 的 optional 强隔离档, 同样每 workload 独立 guest kernel)。共享内核的纯 Docker(Daytona 默认档)不足以装下 "会跑不可信内容 + 持有真钱" 的 agent。
- 架构上 broker 模式不变, 只是 broker 后端从 dockerd 换成 Firecracker/Kata orchestrator —— 现在用 broker 抽象就是为了未来这一步零痛切换。

一句话: 不要 DinD/不要 docker.sock; 用 "宿主 launcher + 窄 broker API + 兄弟容器" 拿到 spawn 能力同时零宿主 Claude; 用 egress 白名单 + per-identity 最小凭据 + EC/Sysbox(Mac)/Firecracker(prod) 做纵深防御。
keyInsights: 核心结论: 不需要 docker-in-docker。CEO 'spawn operator 容器' 的正确含义是创建**兄弟容器**(sibling, 经宿主 dockerd), 不是在 CEO 容器内**嵌套**容器。这同时满足 '零宿主 Claude' 和 'CEO 能管理 operator' 两个硬约束。

把裸 docker.sock 挂进容器 = 把宿主 root 交出去。Docker daemon 以 root 跑, 任何能写 socket 的进程都能 `docker run -v /:/host` 挂载宿主全盘 —— 对一个读取不可信网页(indirect prompt injection)且持有真实凭据/钱的 agent, 这是灾难级风险。只读挂载 socket 也**不能**防护, 因为 create/exec API 仍暴露。
`--privileged` DinD 逃逸不需要内核 0-day: privileged 容器可调用 init_module 加载内核模块、直接访问宿主设备和 host-facing kernel 文件系统, 再 pivot 到宿主 shell。属于 '简单逃逸' 类别, 对本项目不可接受。

tecnativa/docker-socket-proxy(及 wollomatic/socket-proxy)能按 HTTP method/路径做 allowlist, 但**无法过滤 POST /containers/create 的请求体参数**, 因此仍可被注入恶意 bind-mount/privileged 配置。高安全场景必须用**自写语义化 broker**(只接受 image+固定安全模板, 拒绝任意 mount/privileged), 不能只靠 socket-proxy。
macOS Docker Desktop 是**单一共享 LinuxKit VM**: 所有容器共享一个 Linux 内核, 容器间隔离 = namespace+cgroup(软隔离), 仅 VM↔macOS 之间是硬(hypervisor)边界。所以 Mac 上 '容器间' 不是强隔离, 一个内核漏洞可波及同 VM 内所有容器。
Sysbox 让 DinD '安全'(user-ns 映射, 容器内 root→宿主非特权, 内层 Docker 与宿主 Docker 完全隔离, 无需 privileged/无需挂 socket), **但 Sysbox 仅支持 Linux, 不支持 macOS 单独安装**。

关键转折: Docker Desktop 的 **Enhanced Container Isolation (ECI) 内部就是用 Sysbox**, 跑在 LinuxKit VM 里, 因此 **ECI 在 macOS Docker Desktop 可用** —— 默认阻止挂 docker.sock、即使 --privileged 也无法 load kernel module/改宿主网络, 容器 root 映射到 VM 内非特权 UID。代价: 需要 **Docker Business 付费订阅**。这是 Mac 测试期最实际的纵深防御。

强隔离技术阶梯(对不可信工作负载): Firecracker microVM(每 session 独立 kernel, KVM 硬隔离, ~125ms 启动, ~5MB 开销, 逃逸需 hypervisor escape — 业界 gold standard, E2B 在用) ≈ Kata Containers(每 workload 独立 guest kernel, Daytona 的 optional 档) > gVisor(用户态内核拦截 syscall, 比容器强但非 VM 级, I/O 有 10-30% 开销) > 纯 runc 容器(共享内核, 最弱)。
对抗 prompt-injection 数据外泄, **网络 egress 白名单是最有效的单点控制**: Simon Willison 的 'lethal trifecta'(私有数据 + 不可信内容 + 对外通信)三者齐备即可被利用; 砍掉第三条(只放行任务必需域名), 注入即使成功也无法把数据/凭据发给攻击者。

真实平台做法佐证架构: E2B=Firecracker microVM 每 sandbox 独立 kernel; Daytona=默认 Docker 容器 + 可选 Kata 强隔离 + 可配 egress 控制 + 合规; cua=ephemeral VM/容器 per-session(Apple Virtualization.Framework / QEMU), 用过即重置。没有一家把生产级不可信 agent 直接放进共享内核裸容器并挂 socket。
凭据与横向移动: 必须 per-identity 最小权限凭据 + 运行时短期注入(不写进 image)+ 可即时吊销; 一个 operator 被攻陷不应能拿到其它身份的凭据或访问其它 operator 容器(无横向移动)。这是 '持有真钱' 场景的硬性要求。

options.name: A. CEO 容器挂裸 docker.sock 直接 spawn operator (DooD, Docker-out-of-Docker)
options.howItWorks: 把宿主 /var/run/docker.sock bind-mount 进 CEO 容器, CEO 内的 docker CLI 直接命令宿主 dockerd 创建 operator 兄弟容器。技术上满足 '零宿主 Claude'(CEO 在容器内)且 operator 是兄弟容器。
options.isolation: 几乎为零 —— 等于把宿主 root 交给 CEO
options.needsPrivileged: 否(但等价于 privileged: socket=宿主 root)
options.macFeasibility: 可行(Mac 上 socket 在 LinuxKit VM 内, 拿到的是 VM root 而非 macOS root, 略降风险但仍是全 VM 失陷)
pros: 实现极简, 是最常见的 'CEO spawn operator' 写法
满足兄弟容器/零宿主 Claude 两个硬约束
Mac 上 socket 只到 LinuxKit VM root, 不直达 macOS
cons: 对一个会读不可信网页 + 持真钱的 agent 是灾难: 一次 prompt injection 即可 `docker run -v /:/host` 挂全盘提权
只读挂载 socket 也不防护(create/exec API 仍在)
CEO 失陷 = 整个 VM 内所有 operator + 凭据全失陷, 无隔离边界
与 '安全的零人公司' 目标根本冲突, 不可用于本项目

options.name: B. 宿主 launcher daemon + 窄 broker API + 兄弟容器 (推荐 Mac 测试期主架构)
options.howItWorks: 宿主跑一个非-Claude 的 launcher daemon, 它独占 dockerd 访问。对 CEO 容器只暴露一个语义化窄 API(create-operator(image, task-template) / stop / logs), 内部用固定安全模板创建 operator 兄弟容器(cap-drop ALL、no-new-privileges、只读 rootfs、seccomp、egress 白名单、per-identity 凭据注入)。CEO 永远拿不到裸 docker 能力, 只能在白名单语义内请求。
options.complexity: 中(需自写 broker, 但逻辑简单)
options.isolation: 中-高(取决于模板严格度; 配 ECI 后显著提升)
options.macFeasibility: 完全可行, 是 Mac 测试期最务实方案
pros: 同时满足两个硬约束: 零宿主 Claude + CEO 能 spawn/manage operator
CEO 失陷无法注入任意 mount/privileged, 攻击面被语义 API 收窄
安全控制(egress、cap、凭据)集中在 broker 强制执行, 单点审计
broker 抽象让未来切到 Firecracker/Kata 零痛(

options.name: C. tecnativa/docker-socket-proxy 限制 socket 后给 CEO
options.howItWorks: 在 CEO 与 dockerd 之间放 docker-socket-proxy, 用环境变量按 HTTP method/路径 allowlist(如允许 POST /containers/create)。比裸 socket 安全, 比自写 broker 省事。
pros: 现成开源, 部署快
能挡掉大量只读/无关 API
比裸 socket 明显更好
cons: 致命短板: 无法过滤 POST /containers/create 的**请求体参数**, 仍可被注入 `-v /:/host` 或 privileged → 一旦放开 create 就等于放开提权
只能做路径/方法级 allowlist, 做不到参数/语义级安全
对 '不可信内容 + 真钱' 场景不够, 只能当 broker 的补充, 不能当主防线

options.name: D. Docker Desktop Enhanced Container Isolation (ECI / Sysbox) 叠加在 B 之上
options.howItWorks: 开启 Docker Desktop ECI(底层 Sysbox): LinuxKit VM 内所有容器自动用 Sysbox runtime, 容器 root→VM 内非特权 UID(user namespace), 默认阻止挂 docker.sock, 即使 --privileged 也无法 load kernel module/改宿主网络/访问宿主设备。
options.complexity: 低(开关 + 配置), 但需付费
options.isolation: 高(在共享内核约束内接近上限)
options.macFeasibility: macOS Docker Desktop 可用, 但需 Docker Business 订阅
pros: macOS 上唯一现成的 'Sysbox 级' 容器加固(Sysbox 本身不单独支持 Mac, 但 ECI 内嵌它)
即使某容器被诱导 --privileged 也无法逃逸到 VM/宿主
默认堵死 docker.sock 滥用, 与零信任目标一致
对现有 docker 工作流透明, 无需改命令
cons: 需要 Docker Business 付费订阅(成本/采购门槛)
仍是共享内核(VM 内一个 kernel), 防的是容器→宿主提权, 不等于 microVM 级 per-session 硬隔离
保护程度随 Docker Desktop 版本而异, 部分组件(extension 等)未覆盖

options.name: E. Firecracker microVM / Kata Containers per-session (推荐生产期目标)
options.howItWorks: broker 后端从 dockerd 换成 Firecracker(KVM microVM, 每 session 独立 guest kernel)或 Kata(每 workload 独立 guest kernel)。每个 agent session 一个 microVM, 逃逸需 hypervisor escape(更难一个量级)。E2B 用 Firecracker, Daytona 提供 Kata 选项。
options.complexity: 高(需 Linux/KVM 主机 + microVM orchestrator + 网络/镜像分发工程)
options.isolation: 最高(per-session 独立 kernel, 硬虚拟化边界)
options.needsPrivileged: 否(KVM 边界本身就是强隔离)
options.macFeasibility: 不适合 Mac 测试期(Firecracker 需 Linux+KVM; Mac Docker Desktop 无法跑); 属于上云/生产阶段
pros: 业界对 '不可信代码/内容' 的 gold standard(E2B/Daytona-Kata 均采用)
单 session 内核漏洞/逃逸被限制在该 microVM, 不波及他人
~125ms 启动 + ~5MB 开销, 兼顾密度与速度
彻底解决共享内核的横向风险
cons: Mac 测试期无法落地(需 Linux+KVM 宿主), 现在不能用
网络(CNI)与镜像分发是 microVM 项目的主要工程难点(启动延迟、egress 成本)
运维复杂度明显高于容器
属于 'broker 后端替换' 的未来步骤, 非当下
